data.title: European markets rise amid corporate earnings flurry, BOE decision ahead
data.description:
London (UKX) +1.02%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.78%

France (CAC:IND) +0.33%

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) climbed 0.56% to 541.59, marking a third consecutive session of gains, boosted by strong corporate results as markets wait for latest monetary policy decision from the Bank of England and more earnings reports.

Coming up in the next session: Eurozone retail sales data expected shortly. BOE decision is the highlight on European side.

On the corporate front, shares of AstraZeneca gained after the company's earnings and revenue topped forecasts, Societe Generale jumped about 7% after reporting a rise in Q4 revenue. Besides, Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY), Volvo (OTCPK:VOLAF), Kering (OTCPK:PPRUF) in focus.

In the bond market: The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points to 4.45%.

Germany's 10-year yield up basis points to 2.38%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point at 4.45%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK).

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: AstraZeneca core EPS surpasses estimates with strong Q4 results; initiates FY outlook
AstraZeneca beats Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• AstraZeneca press release (NASDAQ:AZN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.09 beats by $1.02.
  • Revenue of $14.89B (+23.9% Y/Y) beats by $660M.
  • Guidance: The company issues its Total Revenue and Core EPS guidance for FY 2025 at CER, based on the average foreign exchange rates through 2024.
  • Total Revenue is expected to increase by a high single-digit percentage.
  • Core EPS is expected to increase by a low double-digit percentage.
  • The Core Tax rate is expected to be between 18-22%.

data.title: BBB Foods prices secondary offering at $28.25
Nissan CEO informs Honda chief of intent to end merger talks - report
data.description:
Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) CEO Makoto Uchida has informed his counterpart at Honda (NYSE:HMC) of his intent to end merger talks after the latter proposed making Nissan a subsidiary, Japanese news agency Kyodo reported, citing a source familiar with the matter.

The conversation reportedly happened when Uchida met Honda (NYSE:HMC) CEO Toshihiro Mibe on Thursday at Honda's headquarters in Tokyo.

Japan's third-biggest automaker vehemently opposed Honda's (HMC) proposal of (OTCPK:NSANY) becoming its subsidiary. Honda is the country's second-largest automaker.

The proposal deviates from Honda's (HMC) original plan to merge the two companies, as it believed Nissan's (OTCPK:NSANY) progress in turning around its business was slow. Nissan scaling up its turnaround efforts was a key premise for the deal.

The company, which announced 9,000 layoffs and cut its global production capacity by 20%, did not come up with a detailed restructuring plan that convinced Honda (HMC), according to the report.

At the December press conference confirming the merger talks, Nissan's (OTCPK:NSANY) Uchida had stressed that both companies would be on equal footing in the merger that would've created the world's third-largest automaker by sales.

Mitsubishi Motors (OTCPK:MMTOF), in which Nissan (OTCPK:NSANY) owns a 24% stake, is reportedly leaning towards not joining the merger due to an expected decrease in autonomy.

data.title: SunCar Technology prices $50M follow-on share offering
data.description:
SunCar Technology Group (NASDAQ:SDA) priced its previously announced public offering of ~7.14M shares for total expected gross proceeds of ~$50M.

Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~1.07M shares.

The offering is expected to close on or about February 7, 2025.

SunCar (NASDAQ:SDA) intends to use the net proceeds for working capital and general corporate purposes.

The stock price declined 4% on Wednesday during after-market hours of trading.

data.title: Siemens Healthineers Non-GAAP EPS of €0.51, revenue of €5.48B; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Siemens Healthineers AG press release (OTCPK:SEMHF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.51.
  • Revenue of €5.48B (+5.8% Y/Y).
  • Free cash flow reached around €810 million, more than three times as high as in the prior-year quarter.
  • Equipment order intake in the first quarter markedly exceeded equipment revenue. The equipment book-to-bill ratio was an excellent 1.21.
  • Adjusted EBIT rose 11% to around €822 million in the first quarter from the prior-year period.
  • Outlook: For fiscal year 2025, we continue to expect comparable revenue growth of between 5% and 6% over fiscal year 2024. The expectation for adjusted basic earnings per share remains unchanged in the range of €2.35 to €2.50.
